body: PR adds code-review rules (.claude/rules/code-review.md) and enables the cru-plugins marketplace (.claude/settings.json). Both files are Critical-risk (review pipeline config). No must-fix issues. The rules are thorough, well-aligned with CLAUDE.md, and previous review findings have been addressed. Two minor nice-to-haves: marketplace version pinning and Apollo risk deduplication.

comments.body: [Nice-to-have] Consider pinning the marketplace to a specific tag or commit SHA to prevent supply-chain drift if the upstream repo is compromised.

[Nice-to-have] Apollo files appear in High-Risk (+2), Special Pattern Detection (+2 for cache.ts), Security triggers, and Data Integrity triggers. The layering is intentional but could we add a note clarifying that risk scores don't stack across sections?
